title: Chandragiri Fort
tagline: Chandragiri Fort — heritage city of Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Chandragiri Fort is a historical fort located near Tirupati in Andhra Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Chandragiri Fort is a historical fort located near Tirupati in Andhra Pradesh, India. The fort, with its rectangular bastions and steep moat, was built around 1000AD during the reign of Immadi Narasimha Yadavaraya, who ruled from Narayanavanam.

topPlaces.name: Chandragiri
topPlaces.description: Chandragiri is a village located in Chandragiri mandal, Tirupati district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is a part of Tirupati Urban Development Authority It is the mandal headquarters of Chandragiri mandal in Tirupati revenue division.

topPlaces.name: Kalyana Venkateswara Temple, Srinivasamangapuram
topPlaces.description: Kalyana Venkateswara Temple is an ancient Hindu temple at Srinivasamangapuram in Tirupati. It is located in Tirupati District of Andhra Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Pullaiahgaripalli
topPlaces.description: Pullaiahgaripalli (P.G.Palli) is a neighbourhood village of Tirupati. It is a part of Tirupati urban agglomeration and located in Chandragiri mandal.

topPlaces.name: Naravaripalle
topPlaces.description: Naravaripalle is a village near Tirupati. It is located in Chandragiri mandal in Tirupati district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Cherlopalle
topPlaces.description: Cherlopalle is a neighbourhood located in Tirupati city in Tirupati district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It forms a part of Tirupati urban agglomeration and is located in Tirupati revenue division.

topPlaces.name: Sri Venkateswara Zoological Park
topPlaces.description: Sri Venkateswara Zoological Park is located in Tirupati, Andhra Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Kalyani Dam
topPlaces.description: The Kalyani Dam is a gravity dam constructed across the Swarnamukhi river at Tirupati city and located in Tirupati District of Andhra Pradesh, India. This dam is one of the major sources of water supply for Tirupati city and its catchment areas.

topPlaces.name: Ghat Roads
topPlaces.description: Ghat Roads are access routes into the mountainous Western and Eastern Ghats, mountain ranges of the Indian subcontinent. These roads are remarkable feats of engineering, and most were constructed during the British Raj.

items.note: Chandragiri is a village located in Chandragiri mandal, Tirupati district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is a part of Tirupati Urban Development Authority It is the mandal headquarters of Chandragiri mandal in Tirupati revenue division.
Kalyana Venkateswara Temple is an ancient Hindu temple at Srinivasamangapuram in Tirupati. It is located in Tirupati District of Andhra Pradesh, India.
Pullaiahgaripalli (P.G.Palli) is a neighbourhood village of Tirupati. It is a part of Tirupati urban agglomeration and located in Chandragiri mandal.
Naravaripalle is a village near Tirupati. It is located in Chandragiri mandal in Tirupati district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh.

Cherlopalle is a neighbourhood located in Tirupati city in Tirupati district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It forms a part of Tirupati urban agglomeration and is located in Tirupati revenue division.
Sri Venkateswara Zoological Park is located in Tirupati, Andhra Pradesh, India.

The Kalyani Dam is a gravity dam constructed across the Swarnamukhi river at Tirupati city and located in Tirupati District of Andhra Pradesh, India. This dam is one of the major sources of water supply for Tirupati city and its catchment areas.

Ghat Roads are access routes into the mountainous Western and Eastern Ghats, mountain ranges of the Indian subcontinent. These roads are remarkable feats of engineering, and most were constructed during the British Raj.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chandragiri Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chandragiri Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chandragiri Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tirupati Airport (26 km) and the nearest railway station is Tirupati Railway Station (14 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chandragiri Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chandragiri Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chandragiri Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Chandragiri Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chandragiri Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chandragiri, Kalyana Venkateswara Temple, Srinivasamangapuram, Pullaiahgaripalli, Naravaripalle, Cherlopalle.
